catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results — first quarter as combined Devon + Coterra
catalysts.why: First full earnings report since the Devon/Coterra merger closed May 7, 2026, so updated combined guidance, synergy capture and capital-return plans could materially re-rate the stock.
catalysts.take: First combined Devon+Coterra print should showcase synergy capture and a beefed-up capital-return framework, a likely re-rating positive unless oil tape weakens guidance.

catalysts.title: 2026 Annual Shareholder Meeting (first post-merger)
catalysts.why: First annual meeting as the enlarged Devon+Coterra entity — board/governance and say-on-pay votes plus any strategic commentary set the tone for the combined company.
catalysts.take: Routine first post-merger annual meeting; governance and say-on-pay votes rarely move a large-cap E&P absent a surprise strategic announcement.

catalysts.title: Q2 FY2026 results (quarter ended ~Aug 1)
catalysts.why: Read on comps, multi-price ('3.0') rollout traction and tariff/margin pressure as a leaner pure-play Dollar Tree post Family Dollar divestiture, a key driver of the margin-recovery thesis.
catalysts.take: Leaner pure-play Dollar Tree hinges on 3.0 multi-price traction versus persistent tariff/margin pressure, a genuinely balanced setup with two-sided risk.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results + Q3 guide
catalysts.why: Q2 print (guided $1,133-1,153M, +14-16%) and the Q3 outlook test whether ~18% revenue growth and record 631M users are sustaining, the core of the ad-monetization re-rate setup.
catalysts.take: Record users and mid-teens guided growth give room for a beat, but PINS has a history of punishing soft Q3 guides so the bar is real.

catalysts.title: Half Year 2026 earnings
catalysts.why: Company-confirmed H1 results update Directional revenue/backlog, FPSO project milestones and the buyback/dividend trajectory that underpin the cash-return story.
catalysts.take: Company-confirmed H1 with strong FPSO backlog and a steady buyback/dividend cadence skews modestly positive for this cash-return story.

catalysts.title: Q4 & full-year fiscal 2026 results + initial FY2027 guidance
catalysts.why: Fiscal year-end print is the biggest catalyst of BILL's year: it pairs Q4 results with full-year FY2027 guidance and historically a fresh ~$300M buyback authorization, framing the take-rate/TPV growth re-acceleration debate that drives the stock.
catalysts.take: FY-end print plus initial FY27 guide and likely fresh buyback is the year's biggest catalyst, but take-rate/TPV deceleration fears make initial guidance a coin-flip risk.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results — first clean quarter post Global Blue/SmartPay integration
catalysts.why: First mid-year print showing whether the Global Blue (closed Jul 2025) and SmartPay acquisitions are driving the promised revenue synergies and margin accretion, the central bull/bear question on the stock.
catalysts.take: First clean post-Global Blue/SmartPay quarter should evidence revenue synergies and margin accretion, though integration execution remains the key swing factor.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results — billings trend vs. FY26 $300M billings / $50M CFFO guide
catalysts.why: After the failed Agora buy-in and the rejected $17.25 Monument & Cathedral go-private bid, the standalone story rests on whether 15%+ billings growth holds and supports the ~13% trailing dividend yield, both of which this print tests.
catalysts.take: Standalone story after the rejected go-private bid rests on fragile 15%+ billings growth that consumer-newsletter cyclicality makes hard to underwrite.

catalysts.title: Next quarterly (and possible special) dividend declaration
catalysts.why: MarketWise's outsized ~13% trailing yield is the core thesis; the next quarterly declaration (typically alongside earnings, ex-date in the mid-month following the prior May 15 ex-date) and any repeat special is the recurring near-term catalyst for the income-driven shareholder base.
catalysts.take: Continuation of the ~13% yield is largely priced; a maintained declaration is supportive but a special is unlikely to repeat, limiting upside surprise.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results — margins vs. record Brazil/Argentina capex ramp
catalysts.why: After Q1's 13% post-print selloff on margin/investment fears, Q2 is the key test of whether the R$57B Brazil + $3.4B Argentina investment cycle is delivering market-share and 70%+ ad/fintech growth without permanently compressing margins.
catalysts.take: After Q1's margin-driven selloff, expectations are reset lower and MELI's execution track record favors a fintech/ads-led beat showing the investment cycle is paying off.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.why: Q2 guidance calls for revenue of $715-725M (~44% YoY) and adj. EBITDA of $285-295M (+71-77%), so a beat/raise on ad growth, ARPU and Google/OpenAI data-licensing momentum is the key near-term re-rating event.
catalysts.take: Ad growth, ARPU expansion and data-licensing momentum have driven repeated beat-and-raises; the setup favors another, though the high-multiple stock needs the guide to clear a tall bar.

catalysts.title: Potential S&P 500 index inclusion
catalysts.why: Now GAAP-profitable with a ~$20B+ cap and ample liquidity, Reddit meets S&P 500 criteria and an addition at a quarterly rebalance would force large passive buying.
catalysts.take: Reddit qualifies on profitability and size, but S&P timing is unpredictable and inclusion at this specific rebalance is more likely than not to slip, so odds of a positive event now are below even.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.why: After issuing formal Q2 guidance of $3.8-4.1B in consumer-loan marketplace origination volume, Q2 results test whether the 90%+ adj. revenue growth and ~50% EBITDA margins can sustain, the main driver for this newly public blockchain-lending name (date sources conflict between late-July and early-Sept, so treat as estimated).
catalysts.take: Newly public blockchain-lender with 90%+ growth has huge move potential, but lock-up dynamics and unproven durability of origination volumes make the binary roughly even.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings & call
catalysts.why: First full quarter showing monetization of the newly GA 'Athena by Zeta' AI agent (already 60% of platform AI activity in week one) plus progress on the $100M buyback makes the print a key proof point for the AI-driven re-rating.
catalysts.take: Early Athena AI adoption plus buyback progress support the re-rating, though Zeta carries elevated short-seller scrutiny that can amplify any guidance wobble.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.why: Management has guided to its first positive EPS by Q3 and needs to rebuild credibility after a Q1 miss (gross margin fell to 19%), so Q2 results and updated guidance are pivotal for this beaten-down recent IPO.
catalysts.take: After a Q1 margin miss, this beaten-down IPO must rebuild credibility toward promised Q3 profitability; execution risk keeps the odds below even.

catalysts.title: IPO 180-day lock-up expiry
catalysts.why: With the Jan 29-30, 2026 IPO close, a standard 180-day lock-up frees insider/early-investor shares around late July, a potential supply overhang on an already-volatile small-float stock (exact date paywalled in the prospectus).
catalysts.take: A 180-day lock-up expiry on a volatile small-float space name is a near-pure supply overhang that typically pressures the shares.

catalysts.title: ALL.SPACE ($355M) acquisition close
catalysts.why: Closing the $355M cash-and-stock ALL.SPACE deal (up to 5.9M York shares) expands York into multi-orbit satcom terminals but is subject to regulatory approval and adds dilution, making the close a clear catalyst.
catalysts.take: Closing ALL.SPACE expands York's satcom reach but adds share dilution and regulatory risk, making the deal close a balanced rather than clearly positive event.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results - first quarter consolidating Legend
catalysts.why: First print to show the $1.2B Legend media acquisition (closed Apr 30) in the numbers, with management guiding to ~$185M group revenue and ~$45M adj. EBITDA, testing the media/advertising synergy thesis.
catalysts.take: First quarter consolidating Legend with a clear ~$185M revenue / ~$45M EBITDA guide gives a visible bar Genius has tended to meet, favoring a positive media-synergy read.

catalysts.title: NFL season start - peak revenue and data product ramp
catalysts.why: The new NFL season is Genius's single largest revenue driver, so the September kickoff begins the seasonally strongest stretch and any new betting/media product traction shows up here.
catalysts.take: NFL kickoff begins the seasonally strongest revenue stretch and any new betting/media product traction is upside, though it's a gradual ramp rather than a single print.

catalysts.title: Half-Year 2026 results
catalysts.why: Key checkpoint on the QUVIVIQ (daridorexant) commercial ramp - guided to ~50% North America/Europe sales growth - and cash runway, which drive the equity story for this turnaround-stage pharma.
catalysts.take: QUVIVIQ ramp and cash runway are the whole story for this turnaround pharma; insomnia-drug uptake has historically disappointed versus guidance, keeping odds just under even.

catalysts.title: Lucerastat Phase 3 program initiation (Fabry disease)
catalysts.why: Starting the Phase 3 for a potential first oral therapy for all Fabry patients adds a major new pipeline value driver and signals R&D execution.
catalysts.take: Initiating Fabry Phase 3 adds a fresh pipeline value driver and signals R&D execution, a low-cost positive optionality even if commercialization is years away.

catalysts.title: Q3 FY2026 results - Juniper integration quarter
catalysts.why: First full quarter consolidating the $14B Juniper acquisition with networking now central to HPE's AI strategy; guidance was Q3 revenue of $11.5-12.1B, so AI-server and networking margins are the swing factor.
catalysts.take: First full Juniper-consolidated quarter with networking central to the AI story should help, though AI-server margin dilution remains the swing risk to the print.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results
catalysts.why: After Q1 backlog strength and a raised FY26 EPS outlook to $12.65-12.95, this print tests whether R&D Solutions bookings and biotech funding momentum are sustaining, with Q2 guided to $4.28-4.34B revenue.
catalysts.take: Backlog strength and a raised FY26 EPS outlook give momentum, but lingering biotech-funding sensitivity keeps this from being a high-conviction beat.

catalysts.title: PDUFA: low-dose estrogen weekly contraceptive patch
catalysts.why: An FDA approval would validate Viatris's pivot toward branded/specialty assets and add a novel women's-health product to the portfolio, a re-rating catalyst for a stock the market treats as a declining generics name.
catalysts.take: A confirmed-date PDUFA on a well-supported low-dose contraceptive patch is more likely to approve than not and would validate the specialty-shift re-rating thesis.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings + guidance reiteration
catalysts.why: Investors watch for base-business stabilization, new-product revenue ramp and any guidance change after a year of regulatory/Indore-facility overhangs; Q2 2025 was reported Aug 7, so a similar early-August date is likely.
catalysts.take: Base-business stabilization and new-product ramp are the focus, but Indore-facility and generics-erosion overhangs make guidance a balanced two-sided event.

catalysts.title: PDUFA: MR-141 (phentolamine 0.75%) presbyopia eye drop
catalysts.why: Approval of a presbyopia drop (positive Phase 3 VEGA-2/VEGA-3) would open a large eye-care market and further the specialty-shift thesis; just outside the 3-month window but a known near-term binary.
catalysts.take: Positive VEGA-2/VEGA-3 Phase 3 data de-risks the MR-141 presbyopia PDUFA, and approval opens a large eye-care market, favoring a positive outcome.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.why: Key read on Las Vegas Strip volumes, BetMGM digital trajectory and MGM China/Macau strength, plus capital-return pace via buybacks; date estimated off prior-year cadence and not yet company-confirmed.
catalysts.take: Las Vegas Strip volume softness offsets BetMGM and Macau strength plus aggressive buybacks, leaving a genuinely balanced print.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.why: Focus on OneAmerica retirement-integration accretion (plans moved to Voya in April 2026), Employee Benefits loss-ratio trends and continued double-digit adjusted-EPS growth; date estimated off prior-year cadence, not yet confirmed.
catalysts.take: OneAmerica integration accretion and double-digit adjusted-EPS growth support a modest beat, though Employee Benefits loss-ratio remains the recurring risk.

catalysts.title: Q2 FY2027 earnings (quarter ended Aug 31, 2026)
catalysts.why: First full quarter to test new CEO Keith Barr's four-pillar growth plan and SG&A/reconditioning cost-out story after a strong Q1 FY27 beat; CarMax has company-confirmed the date in its SEC 8-K.
catalysts.take: Following a strong Q1 FY27 beat, the new CEO's cost-out and four-pillar plan have momentum, though used-vehicle affordability and rate sensitivity keep the bar meaningful.

catalysts.title: Strategic Update on four-pillar growth plan
catalysts.why: Management committed to a late-Fall 2026 strategic update detailing milestones/targets behind the new four-pillar plan, a potential framework-and-guidance catalyst; timing is company-indicated but not yet dated (likely just past the Sept window).
catalysts.take: A strategic update with concrete milestones/targets could provide a framework-and-guidance catalyst, but undated timing and execution-show-me risk temper conviction.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings - first full quarter with Anywhere
catalysts.why: First clean quarter showing the merged Compass+Anywhere platform against guidance of $4.0-4.2B revenue and $310-350M adj. EBITDA, the key test of the $225M+ synergy story.
catalysts.take: First clean Compass+Anywhere quarter tests the $225M+ synergy story against a still-soft housing-transaction backdrop, a balanced setup.

catalysts.title: Q4 & full-year fiscal 2026 results
catalysts.why: Year-end print and FY27 guide is the proving ground for the activist-driven cost program, with Starboard pushing to double the cost-savings target to $500M and a 25% EBITDA margin goal.
catalysts.take: Management raising the cost-cut target toward Starboard's $500M ask would reset the margin trajectory and the re-rating case, a likely-positive activist outcome.

catalysts.title: FY27 outlook / activist cost-savings update
catalysts.why: Management's response to Starboard's demand to lift the cost-cut target from $250M to $500M (mostly overhead) could reset the margin trajectory and the stock's re-rating case.
catalysts.take: Management raising the cost-cut target toward Starboard's $500M ask would reset the margin trajectory and the re-rating case, a likely-positive activist outcome.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings - turnaround proof point
catalysts.why: Next read on the turnaround after ~700 store closures and 8% cost cuts drove a 520bp gross-margin jump; tests the wide $2.40-3.10 adj. EPS guide and whether margin gains are durable.
catalysts.take: Margin gains from store closures and cost cuts are encouraging, but the very wide EPS guide signals low visibility and the durability of the turnaround is unproven.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.why: Cyclical print on TiO2/Titanium Technologies and Thermal & Specialized Solutions demand, with first commentary on the just-announced $112.5M EPA PFAS settlement's cash and reserve impact.
catalysts.take: Cyclical TiO2 demand remains soft and first commentary on the PFAS settlement's cash impact adds uncertainty, leaving the print modestly skewed to the downside.

catalysts.title: EPA PFAS consent decree comment period closes
catalysts.why: The proposed consent decree (announced Jun 24, 2026) is in a 30-day public comment window before court approval; resolution removes a major federal PFAS overhang for a capped $112.5M and clarifies the liability path.
catalysts.take: Closing the comment window toward a capped $112.5M consent decree removes a major federal PFAS overhang and clarifies the liability path, a net de-risking positive.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results (peak summer season quarter)
catalysts.why: Q2 is the seasonal peak for amusement parks and the first clean read on post-merger attendance/per-cap trends and EBITDA synergy progress, making it the most stock-moving print of the year.
catalysts.take: Peak-season Q2 is the year's most stock-moving print and the first clean read on post-merger attendance/per-cap and synergies, but weather and consumer softness make it genuinely two-sided.

catalysts.title: Six Flags La Ronde (Montreal) sale to EPR Properties closes
catalysts.why: Final piece of the ~$331M seven-park EPR divestiture; closing brings additional after-tax cash for debt paydown and completes the portfolio-optimization narrative.
catalysts.take: Closing the La Ronde sale completes the EPR divestiture and brings after-tax cash for debt paydown, a modest, largely-expected positive.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results
catalysts.why: Key check on whether the multi-year turnaround is stabilizing sales declines and margins under the new CFO; consensus is depressed so a beat/raise could re-rate the stock.
catalysts.take: Depressed consensus sets a low bar where any sign of stabilizing sales and margins under the new CFO could re-rate the stock, though the turnaround has repeatedly stalled.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results — first full quarter post-CHP merger
catalysts.why: First clean quarter fully reflecting the $1.8B CNL Healthcare (CHP) merger close (Mar 11), so it sets the new run-rate revenue/EBITDA and occupancy trajectory the post-deal thesis hinges on.
catalysts.take: First clean post-CHP-merger quarter sets the new run-rate with senior-housing occupancy tailwinds supportive, but integration noise can muddy the print.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results
catalysts.why: Read on Puerto Rico stabilization, Panama/Caribbean momentum and free-cash-flow/leverage trends, plus likely management commentary on the Costa Rica appeal and the new $500M preferred-stock plan.
catalysts.take: Puerto Rico stabilization and FCF/leverage trends are the swing factors; LILA's chronic execution and high-debt overhang keep the print skewed slightly negative.

catalysts.title: Costa Rica regulator (SUTEL) ruling on Millicom merger appeal
catalysts.why: Binary regulatory outcome — approval would revive the value-accretive Costa Rica combination with Millicom; rejection forces LILA to pursue alternatives, so the ruling is a discrete catalyst either way.
catalysts.take: A binary SUTEL ruling on the Millicom appeal is high-impact either way, but the regulatory outcome is genuinely uncertain so I hold neutral at even odds.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results
catalysts.why: First read on whether the Braintree manufacturing restart and back-half SurgiMend recovery are tracking to the raised 2026 EPS guide, the central debate in the turnaround.
catalysts.take: Whether the Braintree restart and SurgiMend recovery track the raised guide is the central turnaround debate, and Integra's history of operational misses makes it a true coin-flip.

catalysts.title: Braintree go-live + Q3 SurgiMend inventory build ahead of Q4 relaunch
catalysts.why: Braintree coming online (end-June) and the Q3 inventory build are the gating steps to the Q4 SurgiMend/PriMatrix relaunch that underpins the 2026-27 revenue re-acceleration thesis.
catalysts.take: Braintree go-live and Q3 inventory build are the gating steps to the Q4 relaunch that underpins re-acceleration; on-track execution is a positive but the proof is the later relaunch.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results
catalysts.why: Key update on the cost-out/margin-expansion program and respiratory-normalized base business, the swing factors for the de-levering story.
catalysts.take: Cost-out/margin expansion plus a respiratory-normalized base supports the de-levering story, though revenue growth remains soft enough to keep conviction moderate.

catalysts.title: Q2 / H1 2026 results (BIST/KAP filing)
catalysts.why: Continuation of the steep pension-AUM and net-income growth (1Q26 net income +318% y/y) that has been driving the re-rating in a high-real-rate Turkish environment.
catalysts.take: Steep pension-AUM and net-income growth in a high-real-rate Turkish environment have been driving the re-rating and momentum favors another strong H1 print.

catalysts.title: Pilot Mountain Pre-Feasibility Study (PFS) completion/publication
catalysts.why: The PFS is the single largest de-risking event, setting the first hard economics (capex/NPV/IRR) for the largest undeveloped US tungsten deposit and validating the DPA-backed defense-metal thesis.
catalysts.take: The Pilot Mountain PFS is the single largest de-risking event, setting first hard economics for the largest undeveloped US tungsten deposit and validating the DPA-backed defense thesis.

catalysts.title: Mine Plan of Operations filing to BLM (initiates NEPA permitting)
catalysts.why: Submitting the Plan of Operations starts the federal NEPA permitting clock, a tangible move from explorer toward developer that the market watches for project timeline credibility.
catalysts.take: Filing the Mine Plan of Operations starts the NEPA clock and is a tangible explorer-to-developer milestone the market rewards, albeit incremental versus the PFS.

catalysts.title: Maiden Gunmetal Zone drilling + post-PFS exploration update
catalysts.why: First-ever drill results at the Gunmetal Zone plus a comprehensive post-PFS exploration update offer resource-expansion newsflow that can extend mine life beyond the PFS base case.
catalysts.take: Maiden Gunmetal Zone drilling offers resource-expansion optionality and exploration newsflow, but early drill results are inherently uncertain and lower-impact than the PFS.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings (before market open)
catalysts.why: Fiverr guided Q2 revenue down 13% to 5% YoY ($95-103M) so the print and any stabilization in its AI-transition freelance marketplace is the key value/re-rate setup.
catalysts.take: Guidance implies revenue down 13% to up 5% and the entire debate is whether the AI transition stabilizes the marketplace; secular pressure keeps the print skewed slightly negative.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 results & conference call (company-confirmed)
catalysts.why: Company-confirmed Q2 print is the next catalyst to test whether acquisition synergies and share gains can offset a weak new-vehicle/auto-transport volume backdrop at a sub-$8 stock.
catalysts.take: Acquisition synergies and share gains must offset a weak new-vehicle/auto-transport volume backdrop at this sub-$8 stock, a setup that tilts modestly negative.

catalysts.title: H1 2026 interim results (six months ended 30 Jun 2026)
catalysts.why: Half-year results will show whether the Search segment margin recovery holds and quantify the Google AI-Overviews hit to its ad-monetization business, the central debate on the stock.
catalysts.take: H1 results will quantify the Google AI-Overviews hit to ad monetization, a structural headwind that makes a clean Search-margin recovery hard to demonstrate.

catalysts.title: Q2 FY2027 results (quarter ending 31 Jul 2026) via 8-K + shareholder letter
catalysts.why: With live calls/guidance suspended, the Q2 8-K and shareholder letter are the next read on ARR (~$441M) and EBITDA margin trajectory post-Hearsay integration, the core of the value case.
catalysts.take: With live calls suspended, the 8-K and shareholder letter update ARR and margin trajectory post-Hearsay; flat ARR keeps this a balanced value-stock print.

catalysts.title: Q2 FY2027 earnings — Blackwell/GB300 ramp and data-center guide
catalysts.why: The print is the cleanest read on Blackwell-Ultra/GB300 ramp, hyperscaler capex durability and China contribution; guidance sets the tone for the whole AI-infra complex.
catalysts.take: Demand still outstrips supply on Blackwell and hyperscaler capex keeps surprising up, so beat-and-raise remains base case — but a stretched buy-side bar and any China/gross-margin wobble caps the upside skew.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — DAU trajectory vs. Google AI-Overviews traffic overhang
catalysts.why: Stock is whipsawing on the Google AI-search debate; Q2 (guide $715-725M rev, ~44% YoY) tests whether logged-in DAU growth and data-licensing economics outweigh the referral-traffic risk.
catalysts.source: https://marketchameleon.com/Overview/RDDT/Earnings/Earnings-Dates/ (confirmed Aug 11, 2026); Q2 guide from Q1 call
catalysts.take: Similarweb DAU tracking (DA Davidson ~20% YoY vs ~18% cons) and AI data-licensing optionality argue for a beat, but RDDT is a high-beta name where the Google traffic narrative can override good fundamentals, so conviction is moderate.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — first full combined Devon+Coterra quarter, $1B synergy proof
catalysts.why: With the Coterra merger closed ~May 2026, this is the first clean combined-entity print; market needs evidence on synergy capture, pro-forma FCF/buyback and production cadence to re-rate the deal.
catalysts.source: https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/DVN/earnings/ (date estimated off Q2'25 Aug 5 pattern); merger close per Devon IR
catalysts.take: Integration execution and shareholder-return cadence should be solid, but oil tape is the swing factor and merger-arb/dilution overhang plus a soft crude backdrop keep this close to a coin-flip directionally.

catalysts.title: Advancing AI 2026 event — MI450/Helios rack-scale and EPYC Zen 6 reveal
catalysts.why: This is the nearest high-impact catalyst (ahead of Aug 4 earnings): MI450 sampling/Helios ramp detail and any named hyperscaler commitments are the crux of the bull case that AMD takes the next AI-infra wave from NVDA.
catalysts.take: Lisa Su events historically deliver credible roadmaps and Helios/MI450 momentum plus multi-GW pipeline talk should land well, but the stock already prices a lot of MI450 optimism so it needs a marquee customer commit to move materially higher.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — ~$11.2B revenue guide, data-center AI GPU ramp
catalysts.why: Confirms whether the data-center AI ramp (guided +46% YoY at midpoint) is converting to revenue and margin; secondary to the July product event but a key fundamental checkpoint.
catalysts.source: https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/amd/earnings (confirmed Aug 4, 2026); Q2 guide ~$11.2B +/- $300M from Q1 release
catalysts.take: Data-center momentum and a credible H2 ramp setup favor a beat, but back-half-loaded MI400 economics and a high bar make this a more modest mover than the product event.

catalysts.title: Atacicept PDUFA — accelerated-approval decision in IgA nephropathy
catalysts.why: This is the company-defining binary: FDA accelerated-approval decision on lead asset atacicept (BAFF/APRIL inhibitor) for IgAN, enabling a mid-2026 US commercial launch into a large, competitive nephrology market.
catalysts.take: Strong ORIGIN proteinuria data (46% reduction), Breakthrough Therapy designation, Priority Review and FDA alignment on an earlier eGFR analysis all point to approval — my PoS-style estimate is ~85%, with residual risk on label/CRL and a 'sell-the-news' reaction once de-risked.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — BaaS/embedded-finance momentum and margin turn
catalysts.why: Post-turnaround/consent-order context makes each print pivotal; Q2 (cons ~$541M rev, ~$0.38 EPS) tests whether BaaS partner ramps and cost discipline are restoring earnings power.
catalysts.take: GDOT is a small-cap with a volatile post-print track record and lumpy BaaS economics; the turnaround optionality is real but execution and regulatory-cost noise make the near-term reaction genuinely two-sided.

catalysts.title: Q3 FY2026 earnings — US Receivable Purchase Program ramp toward $1B target
catalysts.why: The whole thesis is the US RPP scaling; management targets 'several-fold' US asset growth and ~$1B in the Structured Receivable Program in FY26, so each quarter's funded-balance trajectory is the catalyst that drives the re-rate.
catalysts.source: https://www.prnewswire.com (VersaBank Q3 results; US RPP target $1B in 2026, surpassed $290M FY25 target) — Q3 FY26 date estimated off fiscal-Q reporting cadence
catalysts.take: US RPP onboarding (largest partner signed, $310M funded in FY25) supports continued sequential asset growth and operating leverage, but timing of partner funding is lumpy and a slower-than-promised ramp would hit a richly-narrative small-cap hard.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — peak-season margins, market-share gains and synergy uplift
catalysts.why: Q2 is the seasonal volume peak for resi brokerage; print (guide $4.0-4.2B rev, $310-350M adj. EBITDA) tests share-gain momentum, raised synergy targets and FCF against a still-soft existing-home-sales backdrop.
catalysts.source: https://investors.compass.com (Q2'26 guide from May Q1 call); date estimated off Q2'25 report on Jul 30, 2025
catalysts.take: Compass keeps taking share (record ~6%+) and the seasonally strong quarter plus raised synergy target favor a beat, but the read-through is hostage to a weak housing-transaction tape, so the EBITDA guide reaction matters more than the headline.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — record June volumes across equities, options & prediction markets
catalysts.why: Robinhood already flagged in filings that it is operating from a 'position of business strength' with RECORD average daily trading volumes so far in June across equities, options and prediction markets. Prediction markets are now the fastest-growing product line by revenue (9B contracts, >1M customers in year one; Bernstein sees ~$586M PM revenue in 2026, +286%). Add tokenized stocks (SpaceX/OpenAI exposure for EU), the Susquehanna JV CFTC exchange, and IPO underwriter approval. A high-volume quarter plus new revenue lines sets up a beat-and-raise into Aug 5.
catalysts.source: TipRanks/Zacks earnings calendar (Aug 5, 2026); Robinhood newsroom (PM JV); Robinhood Q1 2026 results filing flagging record June volumes
catalysts.take: Company has pre-signaled record June trading across all lines and PM revenue is inflecting, so a beat is more likely than not, but the stock's ~280% run means a high bar leaves room for a sell-the-news wobble.

catalysts.title: FQ4'26 earnings — GAAP profitability inflection + Affirm Card scaling toward 7.5M / $10B GMV
catalysts.why: Affirm hit GAAP profitability in FQ3'26 with 35% GMV growth, and management guided FQ4 GMV of $13.15-13.45B / revenue $1,080-1,110M with adj. operating margin 27.5-29.5% — and said it is 'incrementally more positive' on FQ4 growth. The Affirm Card is ~60% toward its 7.5M active card / $10B GMV target, a durable secular GMV driver. This is the quarter to confirm the profitability + Card-scaling narrative into FY27 guidance.
catalysts.source: TipRanks earnings (Aug 20, 2026); Affirm FQ3'26 shareholder letter/slides (35% GMV, GAAP profit, FQ4 guide)
catalysts.take: Newly-achieved GAAP profitability plus a raised FQ4 tone and Card momentum favor an upside surprise, though BNPL credit-cost sensitivity and a stretched multiple cap the asymmetry.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — first clean quarter of Deliveroo accretion + ad monetization ramp
catalysts.why: Q2'26 is the first full quarter showing the Deliveroo integration working: Q1 already showed accelerating Deliveroo MAUs, total orders, and Marketplace GOV in the UK/France/Italy, with Deliveroo set to add ~$200M to 2026 adj. EBITDA. Q2 guide is GOV $32.4-33.4B and adj. EBITDA $770-870M, with rising ad monetization and growth beyond restaurants. A beat on GOV plus EBITDA upside despite heavy investment would reframe the 'spend year' bear case.
catalysts.source: DoorDash Q1 2026 8-K and earnings call (Deliveroo lift, Q2 guide); TipRanks/Unusual Whales (Aug 5 est., some sources July 30)
catalysts.take: Deliveroo accretion and ad take-rate are real tailwinds, but management has explicitly framed 2026 as a heavy-spend year, so EBITDA guidance commentary is the swing factor and the date is not yet confirmed.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — location adds, ToastIQ AI attach, and international/enterprise expansion
catalysts.why: Toast guided Q2 recurring GP growth of 22-24% and adj. EBITDA of $185-195M, with FY26 recurring GP growth 21-23% and EBITDA $790-810M. The catalyst is location net-adds plus monetization of ToastIQ (AI) and enterprise/retail/international expansion driving ARPU. A net-location beat combined with EBITDA upside extends the profitable-compounder story; Toast has a record of raising the EBITDA guide intra-year.
catalysts.source: TipRanks earnings (Aug 4, 2026); TIKR Q1 2026 preview / Toast Q1 guidance (GP & EBITDA ranges)
catalysts.take: Consistent location growth plus expanding margins make a guide-raise the base case, but vertical SaaS for restaurants is macro-sensitive on net adds, so the magnitude of upside is moderate.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — beaten-down reset with agentic-commerce (UCP) optionality
catalysts.why: SHOP is down ~33% YTD despite Q1 revenue +34% to $3.17B and GMV +35% to $101B — an asymmetric, de-risked setup into Q2 (guide: high-20s% revenue growth, mid-teens FCF margin). The June 17 Spring '26 Edition shipped 150+ updates and made the Universal Commerce Protocol (co-developed with Google for AI-agent checkout) self-serve with no approval gate, positioning Shopify as agentic-commerce infrastructure. A beat against lowered sentiment plus UCP traction commentary can re-rate the stock.
catalysts.source: Shopify Q1 2026 8-K/results (rev +34%, GMV +35%, Q2 guide); Shopify Spring '26 Edition/UCP (June 17); TipRanks (early-Aug est.)
catalysts.take: The 33% YTD drawdown creates favorable risk/reward and growth is still 30%+, but Shopify trades on durability of margins and any FX/guidance softness has repeatedly triggered sharp moves, so conviction is moderate.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — Mexico turns accretive + Brazil full banking license push
catalysts.why: Management said Mexico reached break-even and becomes ACCRETIVE to earnings from Q2 2026 onward, having hit 15M customers as the #3 financial institution there. Combined with the intent to obtain a full Brazil banking license in 2026 (lower funding costs, broader product set), Q2 is the quarter where the Mexico drag flips to a tailwind. Continued ARPAC expansion and credit normalization make an EPS upside surprise plausible into the print.
catalysts.source: Nu Q1 2026 6-K/results (Mexico break-even, accretive from Q2'26; Brazil license intent); TipRanks/Investing.com (Aug 13-18 est.)
catalysts.take: The Mexico break-even-to-accretive flip plus Brazil license optionality is a clean inflection, but EM/FX and Brazil rate-cycle credit risk inject volatility, and the exact date is not yet confirmed.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — guided peak-quarter EPS ramp on acetyl pricing
catalysts.why: Management explicitly guided Q2 adjusted EPS of $2.00-2.40 vs $0.85 in Q1, calling Q2 the peak quarter of 2026, driven by realized Acetyl Chain price increases and seasonal Engineered Materials demand. Hitting (or beating) that guide confirms the earnings turn on a heavily de-rated, high-financial-leverage name and supports the raised FCF target.
catalysts.take: A self-help pricing story into a guided peak quarter on a beaten-down, high-leverage chemical name skews payoff favorably, though execution on acetyl price realization is the swing factor.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings + aluminum mill third cold mill qualifying in July
catalysts.why: STLD pre-announced Q2 EPS of $3.51-3.55 vs $2.78 in Q1 on stronger steel metal margins, and the fabrication backlog runs ~40% above last year into 2027. The forward catalyst is the third cold mill at the Columbus aluminum flat-rolled mill beginning to qualify material in July, derisking the multi-billion aluminum growth platform.
catalysts.source: STLD Q2 2026 guidance 8-K, Jun 17 2026 (sec.gov, streetinsider.com, stocktitan.net)
catalysts.take: The pre-announced beat is largely priced, so upside hinges on aluminum ramp commentary and buyback/capital-return tone rather than the steel print itself.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — USPI ambulatory growth driving another guide raise
catalysts.why: Tenet has a consistent pattern of Q2 beats and full-year guidance raises, powered by USPI, the largest US ambulatory surgery platform, plus disciplined cost control and acquisitions. Q1 same-facility revenue grew double digits; another strong volume quarter plus a raise would extend the re-rating.
catalysts.source: Tenet press release setting July 24 date (businesswire.com); Q1 2026 8-K (sec.gov)
catalysts.take: High-margin ASC mix shift plus a well-established beat-and-raise cadence make a guidance bump the base case, with payer/volume softness the only real downside risk.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — Gas Power orders (10-15 GW) and likely guide raise
catalysts.why: Management guided 10-15 GW of Gas Power equipment orders for Q2; meeting or beating confirms sustained pricing/demand as slots tighten into 2029-30, with a path to 110+ GW combined backlog by year-end. After a Q1 raise and 14% post-print pop, another beat-and-raise on data-center-driven power demand keeps momentum.
catalysts.source: GE Vernova Q1 2026 release/transcript (gevernova.com, fool.com, tikr.com)
catalysts.take: Order momentum and a recent raise set up another positive print, but the stock already discounts strong execution, so the bar on GW orders and margin is high.

catalysts.title: Q2 earnings + DOE $900M HALEU task-order/Piketon expansion progress
catalysts.why: Centrus is the only US-owned enricher, with a $3.9B backlog, $1.9B cash and a January DOE selection for a ~$900M task order to build commercial-scale HALEU at Piketon. Q2 results (plus any finalization/funding milestone on the HALEU expansion and DOE/NNSA sole-source intent) are the next reads on this policy-driven re-rating story.
catalysts.source: Centrus Q1 2026 results/8-K (prnewswire.com, sec.gov); DOE award (simplywall.st)
catalysts.take: Powerful policy/scarcity tailwind and government backing skew bullish, but the stock is momentum-rich and award timing/contract negotiation introduces meaningful binary risk.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 earnings — Transco/LNG/data-center backlog and project adds
catalysts.why: Williams' expansion backlog jumped from $11.8B to $15.5B with Transco, LNG-export and AI data-center power projects; multiple banks (GS, Jefferies, RBC, BofA, MS) recently upgraded and raised targets. Q2 is seasonally soft on EBITDA, so the catalyst is forward commentary, new project sanctioning and a 2H ramp narrative rather than the headline print.
catalysts.take: Secular gas-demand and data-center tailwinds plus fresh Street upgrades support a re-rating, but seasonally weak Q2 EBITDA means the move depends on guidance and new project announcements, not the quarter.

catalysts.title: OPERA-01 Phase 3 topline (palazestrant monotherapy, ER+/HER2- 2L/3L metastatic breast cancer)
catalysts.why: Pivotal Phase 3 PFS readout for an oral CERAN/SERD still ahead; primary completion ~June 2026, topline expected fall 2026.
catalysts.edge: At ~$830M cap the market prices OPERA-01 like a coin-flip skewed to failure, but Phase 1/2 monotherapy mPFS (13.8mo ESR1-mut, 9.2mo wild-type) and a dual ESR1-mut/all-comer primary design give a better-than-implied shot, with multi-bagger re-rate room if the ESR1-mut arm clears.
catalysts.take: Class effect from elacestrant validates SERD activity in ESR1-mut; palazestrant's complete antagonism plus real Phase 2 PFS supports >55% PoS on the co-primary ESR1-mut population. Market scar tissue from prior all-comer SERD misses over-discounts the asset; a clean ESR1-mut win alone justifies a sharp re-rate from a de-rated base.

catalysts.title: LUCIDITY Phase 3 topline (avexitide, GLP-1 receptor antagonist, post-bariatric hypoglycemia)
catalysts.why: Enrollment complete (78 pts); Phase 3 topline on the FDA-agreed Level 2/3 hypoglycemic-event endpoint expected Q3 2026.
catalysts.edge: Five prior statistically significant Phase 1/2 trials plus Breakthrough Therapy and an FDA-agreed endpoint make avexitide one of the most de-risked-on-the-science Phase 3s of 2026, yet AMLX still trades under the ALS-failure cloud, leaving a real probability gap.
catalysts.take: Mechanism (GLP-1 receptor antagonism blunting reactive hypoglycemia) is replicated across 5 trials; replication risk is low and PoS sits ~70%, well above what a post-AMX0035 de-rated stock implies. PBH is an unmet, orphan-like niche with clean pricing power; the AMX0035 ALS scar is causing the market to mistakenly discount a mechanistically unrelated, well-replicated asset.

catalysts.title: CATT1 Phase 3 topline (cadisegliatin, oral liver-selective glucokinase activator, type 1 diabetes)
catalysts.why: Enrollment completing Q3 2026 (~150 pts); first-in-class oral T1D adjunct Phase 3 topline expected H2 2026, funded through readout ($98M cash).
catalysts.edge: Nano-cap with Breakthrough designation and Phase 2 SimpliciT-1 showing HbA1c improvement plus fewer severe hypoglycemia events, yet the readout is almost entirely unpriced, so even a modest probability-weighted success implies large upside.
catalysts.take: Glucokinase-activator hepatoselectivity de-risks the historical hypoglycemia/lipid liabilities of the class; Phase 2 directionally hit both efficacy and the severe-hypo safety signal. PoS ~55% on a confirmatory design, but at this de-rated cap the catalyst is priced near zero, giving the widest asymmetry in the basket.

catalysts.title: ENHANCE-CD Phase 2b induction topline (obefazimod, oral miR-124 enhancer, Crohn's disease)
catalysts.why: 12-week Crohn's induction proof-of-concept readout expected late 2026, the next mechanism-expanding catalyst after positive UC Phase 3 ABTECT.
catalysts.edge: Obefazimod already hit positive Phase 3 induction AND maintenance in ulcerative colitis, heavily validating the oral miR-124 mechanism in IBD, yet the Crohn's Phase 2b option is largely unpriced as a separate, additive indication.
catalysts.take: Cross-IBD read-through from a fully positive UC Phase 3 program plus emerging anti-fibrotic preclinical data raises Crohn's PoS to ~58%, above what the market credits for a 'Phase 2' label on a de-risked mechanism. A positive Crohn's induction roughly doubles the addressable IBD opportunity and would re-rate the platform, not just the asset.

catalysts.title: Deramiocel (CAP-1002) BLA resubmission PDUFA for DMD cardiomyopathy
catalysts.why: FDA lifted the July-2025 CRL, accepted the Class 2 resubmission as complete, and stated it has NOT identified any potential review issues; positive confirmatory Phase 3 HOPE-3 hit its primary (PUL p=0.03) and key cardiac secondary (LVEF p=0.04). Yet ~30% of float is short and Shkreli publicly shorted it, so the stock still carries heavy CRL/efficacy scar tissue.
catalysts.source: Capricor PR (3/10/26 new PDUFA date); HOPE-3 topline; Nasdaq short-interest data; Benzinga (Shkreli short)
catalysts.edge: Market is still pricing the dead CRL plus a high-profile short thesis, but the CRL has been lifted with no new review issues flagged and a positive confirmatory Phase 3 in hand — the fear, not the approval, is mispriced, leaving asymmetric re-rate room into a PRV-eligible approval.
catalysts.take: True PoS ~68%: a resumed review where FDA explicitly flags no review issues plus a confirmatory pivotal that met both primary and key cardiac endpoints is a materially de-risked file; the remaining real risk is the novel allogeneic cell-therapy CMC/efficacy bar and the small-n statistics the bears cite. The crowded short and CRL memory mean even a routine approval should force a sharp re-rate.

catalysts.title: Gedatolisib NDA PDUFA in HR+/HER2-, PIK3CA wild-type advanced breast cancer
catalysts.why: VIKTORIA-1 triplet showed median PFS 9.3 vs 2.0 mo (HR 0.24, p<0.0001) and the doublet 7.4 vs 2.0 mo (HR 0.33); under FDA real-time oncology review with a clean safety read. Sentiment was dented by Roche's giredestrant Phase 3 miss, with commentary lumping gedatolisib into 'oral SERD' class risk.
catalysts.source: JCO VIKTORIA-1 publication; Celcuity PR (FDA acceptance, 7/17/26 PDUFA); ainvest 'binary July bet' note; gedatolisib mechanism (pan-PI3K/mTOR)
catalysts.edge: The bear read-across is a mechanism category error — gedatolisib is a pan-PI3K/mTOR (PAM) inhibitor, not a SERD, so Roche's giredestrant SERD failure is irrelevant to its approvability, and that misplaced class fear is suppressing odds on a robust, statistically overwhelming pivotal under real-time review.
catalysts.take: True PoS ~80%: an HR of 0.24 with p<0.0001 and a manageable safety profile in a high-unmet PIK3CA-WT 2L population is about as clean an oncology approval setup as exists, and real-time oncology review signals FDA comfort. The mispricing is sentiment conflating a SERD trial failure with a PAM-pathway drug; correcting that error is the re-rate.

catalysts.title: UX111 gene therapy BLA resubmission PDUFA for Sanfilippo syndrome type A (MPS IIIA)
catalysts.why: The July-2025 CRL was confined to CMC/supplemental manufacturing and facility-inspection observations — not efficacy or safety. Resubmission accepted with a Sep 19, 2026 date; clinical package now includes up to 8 years of follow-up showing durable function gains vs natural-history decline.
catalysts.source: Ultragenyx PRs (CRL 7/25, resubmission acceptance 4/2/26, longer-term data); NeurologyLive
catalysts.edge: A CMC/inspection-only CRL is the most fixable kind of rejection, yet the market is treating the resubmission with generic 'another CRL' fear; with efficacy never in question and reinspection-type issues addressable, the probability of a clean approval is higher than the discount implies.
catalysts.take: True PoS ~72%: efficacy/safety were never the issue, so once the manufacturing and inspection items are resolved the path is comparatively clear; residual risk is purely whether the CMC fixes and facility re-clearance fully satisfy reviewers by the date. As a diversified large-cap the single-asset upside is muted, but the de-risking is underappreciated and PRV-eligible.

catalysts.title: Relutrigine NDA PDUFA for SCN2A/SCN8A developmental and epileptic encephalopathies
catalysts.why: Pivotal EMBOLD trial was stopped early for efficacy; priority review granted; would be the first targeted/disease-modifying therapy in SCN2A/SCN8A DEEs (no approved options today), with PRV eligibility. Overhang is the 'single small pivotal in an ultra-rare population — is one trial enough' concern.
catalysts.source: Praxis PRs (FDA acceptance/priority review, 9/27/26 PDUFA; EMBOLD stopped for efficacy); BioSpace
catalysts.edge: The market prices meaningful single-trial/AdCom risk, but a pivotal stopped early for efficacy in a fatal pediatric disease with zero approved therapies is exactly where FDA has shown maximal flexibility — the 'one trial enough?' fear is overblown given the magnitude of effect and unmet need.
catalysts.take: True PoS ~65%: stopping for efficacy is a strong signal of a large, unambiguous effect, and the regulatory bar bends toward approval in devastating pediatric DEEs with no alternatives. The genuine risk is reliance on a single pivotal and any durability/dosing questions, but the discount for that is too steep, leaving the most room-to-run (low priced_in) of the set.

catalysts.title: Imsidolimab BLA PDUFA for generalized pustular psoriasis (GPP)
catalysts.why: At a ~$358M market cap with the Street fixated on Vanda's legacy neuro franchise and litigation, imsidolimab is essentially a free option; the IL-36R mechanism is already validated by approved spesolimab (Spevigo), and the GEMINI Ph3 program hit hard (53% clear/almost-clear at wk4 vs 13% placebo, published in NEJM Evidence).
catalysts.source: Vanda PR (FDA BLA acceptance, target date Dec 12 2026); NEJM Evidence GEMINI-1/-2 publication; companiesmarketcap/stockanalysis (cap ~$358M)
catalysts.edge: Market caps the whole company near cash and assigns ~zero to a de-risked IL-36 asset (mechanism already FDA-approved via spesolimab) with clean Ph3 data, so an approval into a rare orphan indication with exclusivity to the late-2030s is almost entirely unpriced.
catalysts.take: The probability gap is the disconnect between an ~80% PoS (validated target, two positive pivotals, NEJM-published, orphan disease, no obvious CMC overhang) and a valuation that ascribes virtually nothing to the program because coverage is thin and investors view VNDA as a neuro/litigation story. Risk is a manufacturing/label nitpick rather than efficacy. Asymmetric: small dollars of imsidolimab value vs. a near-cash cap.

catalysts.title: Emcitate (tiratricol) NDA, Priority Review, for MCT8 deficiency
catalysts.why: Stockholm-listed with only ~3-5 analysts and almost no US investor attention, yet tiratricol is already EU-approved (Feb 2025) as the first and only MCT8 deficiency therapy, so the scientific/efficacy risk is largely retired heading into a US Priority Review decision.
catalysts.source: Egetis PR (FDA acceptance + Priority Review, PDUFA Sep 28 2026); EU approval Feb 2025; MarketScreener (3-5 analyst coverage, Nasdaq Stockholm EGTX)
catalysts.edge: A US approval of an already-EU-approved, first-in-disease orphan drug is high-probability but underpriced because the name trades on a Swedish exchange with thin coverage and minimal US biotech-investor mindshare, leaving the US-market opportunity under-discounted.
catalysts.take: EU approval as the first and only MCT8 therapy de-risks efficacy and CMC; the US PDUFA is more a market-access milestone than a binary science event, supporting an ~82% PoS. The mispricing is structural neglect (foreign listing, 3-5 analysts) rather than data doubt, so US approval can force a re-rate as the US TAM gets recognized.

catalysts.title: Molbreevi (molgramostim) BLA, Priority Review, for autoimmune PAP
catalysts.why: A 3-month PDUFA extension (FDA deemed Savara's information-request responses a 'major amendment') spooked a thinly-followed name, but the company explicitly noted the FDA cited no safety, efficacy, or manufacturing concerns, and IMPALA-2 was a clean positive Ph3 in a disease with zero approved therapies.
catalysts.source: Savara PR (PDUFA extended to Nov 22 2026, no safety/efficacy/CMC concerns cited); IMPALA-2 Ph3 (n=164) positive; ATS 2026 data
catalysts.edge: The market read the PDUFA extension as a yellow flag and de-rated, yet the amendment was procedural with no efficacy/safety/CMC issues flagged, so the true approval odds for a first-ever aPAP therapy are higher than the post-extension price implies.
catalysts.take: Procedural extensions on clean Ph3 packages where FDA cites no substantive concerns usually still approve; ~80% PoS. With ~7-8 analysts and a $1.5B cap that already wobbled on the delay, this is more 'partial' than 'low,' but the delay created renewed room as nervous holders trimmed. First-in-class status (no approved aPAP drug) underpins durable re-rate on approval.

catalysts.title: INO-3107 BLA (accelerated approval) for recurrent respiratory papillomatosis (RRP)
catalysts.why: A post-COVID washout name left for dead, with durable single-arm data (81% of patients had fewer surgeries at Yr1; ~54% complete response by Yr3) and Breakthrough/Orphan tags into the first potential DNA-immunotherapy approval, trading well below the optionality of an approval despite genuine FDA pushback on accelerated-approval eligibility.
catalysts.source: Inovio/FDA (BLA accepted, PDUFA Oct 30 2026, standard review, FDA flagged accelerated-approval eligibility as a review issue, no AdCom currently planned); Ph1/2 surgery-reduction & complete-response data
catalysts.edge: Sentiment is washed out and the stock prices near-certain failure after the FDA's eligibility comment, but the durable surgery-reduction and complete-response data give the program a real (~coin-flip) shot, so the risk/reward is skewed because almost no approval scenario is in the price.
catalysts.take: This is the lowest-PoS, highest-asymmetry pick: the FDA's preliminary 'inadequate justification for accelerated approval' note is a genuine overhang, capping PoS near 45%, but the company can engage on a confirmatory-trial path and the clinical signal is strong and durable. With the name effectively orphaned by investors, any approval (or even constructive FDA dialogue) re-rates violently; sized as a small, deliberately speculative lottery ticket.
